text.title: design-tokens-fundamentals Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for design-tokens-fundamentals. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Establish three tiers before generating any tokens: global (raw primitive values) → semantic (intent aliases) → component (scoped overrides).
rule.rationale: Without the hierarchy, semantic tokens hold raw values and component tokens reference semantics directly — both patterns defeat the "update once, apply everywhere" guarantee.

rule.statement: Semantic tokens must never hold raw values; they must always alias a global token using {global.token.path} reference syntax.
rule.rationale: A semantic token holding a raw hex value must be updated in two places when the brand color changes — defeating the entire purpose of the token layer.

rule.statement: Name semantic tokens by intent, not by value: use color.feedback.error, not color.red; use spacing.md, not spacing.16.
rule.rationale: The value may change; the intent should not. Intent-based names remain valid across rebrands and theme changes.

rule.statement: Use a single naming convention across all tokens in a project — kebab-case is the W3C DTCG default; enforce it in CI via Style Dictionary transforms.
rule.rationale: Mixed conventions (camelCase in some tokens, kebab in others) produce incorrect generated output and make token references unreliable across platforms.

rule.statement: Use a single source of truth: either Figma Variables or a tokens JSON file in the repo — never maintain both independently.
rule.rationale: Without a single source, Figma and code diverge within weeks; token-based theming becomes unreliable and designers stop trusting the system.

rule.statement: Treat breaking token renames (not additions) as a major version bump; communicate them ahead of time to all consuming teams.
rule.rationale: Token renames silently break components in all consuming packages; versioning disciplines prevent surprise failures in downstream design systems.

rule.statement: Enforce semantic-references-global rule in CI: run Style Dictionary build as a validation step and fail the pipeline if any semantic token contains a raw value.
rule.rationale: Manual review of token files does not scale; automated enforcement is the only reliable governance mechanism.

rule.statement: Commit the generated CSS/JS token outputs to the repo so consumers do not need build-time Style Dictionary access.
rule.rationale: Consumers building against the token package should not need to install build tools; pre-built outputs ensure consistency.

rule.statement: Add a token changelog discipline: breaking token renames (not additions) require a major version bump and release notes.
rule.rationale: Breaking changes must be communicated; without clear messaging, teams miss breaking changes and their builds fail silently.
